message:
fix(core): JOIN source subquery column resolution via select_result_column_names + remove test diagnostics

Two related changes from continued work on the JOIN/subquery column
inference path, plus cleanup of the temporary diagnostic eprintlns
that were committed earlier in this same batch.

(1) JOIN table source column inference: subquery shapes

Before: when a JOIN's right-hand TableOrSubquery::Subquery was
expanded, column names were taken from infer_select_column_names()
alone and, if that returned a "*" or "alias.*" unresolved wildcard,
a secondary resolve_subquery_star_columns() pass was invoked
against the schema snapshot.  This produced two inconsistent
column-naming paths for the same input and missed column names
inside CTEs referenced by the subquery.

After: switch both the initial column-name collection and the
post-materialization resolution to
self.select_result_column_names(query, &[], &mut Vec::new()).
That helper already knows how to walk CTEs, resolve aliases, and
consult the schema, so it produces the same column names the rest
of the SELECT path uses.  Same call, two sites — cleaner and no
more divergence between "where the subquery gets parsed" and
"where the subquery gets materialized into the join plan".

The fallback for empty column-name lists (materialize with
_c0, _c1, ... generic names scaled to the actual row width) is
retained unchanged.

New test test_join_with_cte_star_subquery_source_preserves_all_columns
builds a tiny two-table JOIN:

  SELECT s.id, s.payload, rhs.tag
  FROM (
      WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS id, 'left' AS payload)
      SELECT * FROM cte
  ) AS s
  JOIN rhs ON rhs.id = s.id;

and asserts all three columns resolve correctly (id=1, payload="left",
tag="r1").  Before the fix, s.payload would resolve to NULL because
the "WITH cte ... SELECT *" shape dropped the cte's aliased column
list during subquery expansion.

(2) Remove test diagnostic eprintlns

The lazy_dirty_insert_after_delete pager-routing test carried a
batch of temporary DEBUG-prefixed eprintln! calls from an earlier
diagnostic pass (committed as "wip(core/tests): diagnostic eprintlns
in lazy_dirty_insert_after_delete path" in this same batch).  Now
that the underlying pager-routing assertion is understood, remove
the eprintlns.  Output is cleaner; test correctness is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
